footerText: ACE, angiotensin-converting enzyme | ADA, anti-drug antibody | ALT, alanine transaminase | aPTT, activated partial thromboplastin time | ASO, antisense oligonucleotide | AST, aspartate transaminase | AUC, area under the concentration-time curve | C1-INH, C1 esterase inhibitor | C4, complement component 4 | CJD, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease | CLCR, creatinine clearance | Cmax, maximum concentration of drug | Ctrough, trough plasma concentration | CYP, cytochrome P450 | DNA, deoxyribonucleic acid | ECG, electrocardiogram | eGFR, estimated glomerular filtration rate | ESRD, end-stage renal disease | FXIIa, activated factor XII | GERD, gastroesophageal reflex disease | HAE, hereditary angioedema | HAE-C1INH, HAE due to C1-INH deficiency | HCP, healthcare professional | HDL, high density lipoproteins | HMWK, high molecular weight kininogen | HRI, host-related impurity | IU, International Units | IV, intravenous | LDL, low density lipoproteins | LLN, lower limit of normal | MRD, maximum recommended dose | mRNA, messenger ribonucleic acid | MSCS, Mean Symptom Complex Severity | NCI-ODWG, National Cancer Institute Organ Dysfunction Working Group | OLE, open-label extension | PD, pharmacodynamics | PGI-C, Patient Reported Global Impression of Change | PGI-S, Patient Global Impression of Severity | PK, pharmacokinetics | Q2W, every 2 weeks | Q4W, every 4 weeks | Q8W, every 8 weeks | rhC1-INH, recombinant human C1 esterase inhibitor | SC, subcutaneous | TEE, thromboembolic event | TEQ, Treatment Effect Questionnaire | Tmax, time to maximum concentration of drug | TOS, Treatment Outcome Score | ULN, upper limit of normal | URTI, upper respiratory tract infection | US, United States | USP, United States Pharmacopeia | VAS, Visual Analog Scale | vCJD, variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease.
